data.title: Novo Nordisk CEO says up to 1.5M Americans are on compounded GLP-1s
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) CEO Mike Doustdar says that up to 1.5M patients in the U.S. could be using compounded versions of GLP-1 drugs, the popular class of weight loss drugs marketed by the company and its rival Eli Lilly (LLY).

Doustdar, who took the company’s leadership in August, added that compounders knew consumer needs better than the pharmaceutical industry initially did, allowing them to entice those who could not afford or were not willing to pay for branded GLP-1s.

"It's not because this one-and-a-half million patients like to have unsafe, knock-off versions of our products...” Reuters reported, quoting Doustdar as saying during a panel discussion at the ongoing J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference in San Francisco on Monday,

“They (compounders) grabbed a part of the consumers that simply were price sensitive to the whole thing.”

The Danish drugmaker lost ~29% in value last year as the company slashed its 2025 outlook multiple times, citing, among other things, competitive pressures from Lilly (LLY) and compounded GLP-1 drugs, the unapproved low-cost versions of the branded product.

Meanwhile, Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), which markets compounded versions of semaglutide, the active ingredient of Novo’s (NVO) Wegovy, has climbed ~25% over the same period.

"There is this surprising element of a group of companies being able to pass FDA, come and sell unsafe, knock-off products in this market," Doustdar said, adding that the company will continue to engage in legal battles against such practices.

data.title: GFL Environmental plans $1bln private offering of senior notes due 2034
data.description:
• GFL Environmental (GFL) on Tuesday said that it is planning to commence a private offering of $1 billion in senior notes due 2034, to be issued by a U.S. subsidiary and guaranteed by GFL and certain other subsidiaries.
  • Proceeds from the offering are expected to repay amounts drawn on its revolving credit facility and for general corporate purposes.
  • The notes will be offered only to qualified institutional buyers in the U.S. and to investors outside the U.S. in compliance with applicable securities regulations.
  • GFL +0.01% premarket to $42.68.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NANO Nuclear signs MoU to advance micro modular reactor deployment in South Korea
data.description:
NANO Nuclear (NNE) +2.3% pre-market Tuesday after saying it signed a memorandum of understanding with DS Dansuk Co. to cooperate on the development, localization, and deployment of micro modular reactor systems in South Korea.

Under the MoU, NANO Nuclear (NNE) said DS Dansuk will serve as its primary local industrial coordinator, supporting site identification, supply chain localization, regulatory engagement, and institutional partnerships in South Korea.

"This MoU opens doors that would otherwise take years to unlock, and it positions NANO Nuclear as a serious long-term participant in Asia’s clean energy future," Chairman and President Jay Yu said.

Separately, NANO Nuclear (NNE) said Monday it signed an MoU with Ameresco (AMRC) to explore the potential integration of NANO's suite of advanced modular microreactors with Ameresco's engineering, procurement, and construction capabilities to deploy the KRONOS MMR Energy System on federal and commercial sites.

data.title: Vericel expects $276 mln revenue in 2025
data.description:
• Vericel Corporation (VCEL) on Tuesday said that it expects preliminary full-year 2025 revenue of about $276 million, with MACI sales accounting for $239.5 million and Burn Care revenue of $36.5 million.
  • The company said fourth-quarter total revenue and MACI revenue grew 23% year on year.
  • Vericel expects gross margins of 74% and adjusted EBITDA margins of 26%, with GAAP net income profitability for the second consecutive year.
  • Vericel reported a cash and investments position of $200 million with no debt and highlighted record MACI implants, trained surgeons, and continued commercial expansion.
  • Concensus for 2025 revenue is $274.11M.
  • VCEL +0.54% premarket to $36.79.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: KLA Corp. rises on back of TD Cowen upgrade
data.description:
KLA Corp. (KLAC) shares rose nearly 3% in premarket trading on Tuesday after TD Cowen upgraded the semiconductor equipment firm.

“With attention centered on memory [wafer fab equipment] amid rising DRAM/NAND pricing, our updated [wafer fab equipment] work points to leading-edge foundry as the fastest-growing pocket of spend on a CY26-27 CAGR basis (20% Vs 15% for memory), lead by TSMC (& Samsung foundry based on their TSLA execution),” TD Cowen analyst Krish Sankar wrote in a note to clients. “We are upgrading KLAC from Hold to Buy & increasing our PT to $1,800 (36x our new CY27 estimates of ~$50, vs Street's $44).”

Delving deeper, Sankar said any wafer fab equipment spend tied to artificial intelligence is still in the “early innings,” which should benefit KLA Corp.

“The pushback is that sustaining a 36x multiple requires conviction in leading-edge DRAM/ foundry [wafer fab equipment] durability beyond CY27,” Sankar posited. “We argue we are still early in the AI-driven capex acceleration phase: AI remains small in wafer-start terms relative to its revenue mix (15% of TSM wafers this year vs. 1/3rd of revs), implying much of the benefit so far has been absorbed by existing leading-edge capacity rather than net-new tool adds - keeping duration/ visibility biased upward from here. On-device AI is an incremental upside lever into CY27 as functionality expands.”

Upcoming catalysts include commentary from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) — which reports fourth-quarter results on Thursday — about capex spending; any progress updates from Intel's (INTC) and Samsung's (SSNLF) foundry operations; and Apple's (AAPL) June developer conference.

data.title: Old Glory Bank to go public through Digital Asset Acquisition merger
data.description:
Digital Asset Acquisition (DAAQ) announced on Tuesday a business combination with the online banking platform Old Glory Bank to create OGB Financial.

Old Glory Bank's pre-money valuation will be $250M.

The Princeton-based special purpose acquisition company said the Texas corporation will be listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol OGB.

The two companies intend to arrange a PIPE (private investment in public equity) or other proceeds of at least $50M for the merger.

The transaction is expected to close at the end of the first quarter or early in the second quarter of 2026.

"We intend for Old Glory Bank to be the first chartered bank to fully integrate crypto into daily banking," said Michael Staw, co-founder and chief innovation officer of the bank.

data.title: SS Innovations International expects $15M in Q4 revenue
data.description:
• SS Innovations International (SSII) announced on Tuesday that the company expects to report Q4 2025 revenue of approximately $15M, up 85% from revenue of $8.1M in Q4 2024. SSi Mantra installations of 37, up 68% from 22 installations in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • For the FY 2025, the company expects to report revenue of approximately $43M, up 108% from revenue of $20.6M in 2024. SSi Mantra installations of 103, up 119% from 47 installations in 2024.
  • The SSi Mantra cumulative installed base totaled 168 as of December 31, 2025, up 158% from 65 as of December 31, 2024.
  • The company expects to report its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results before the end of February.
  • SSII +3.17% premarket to $5.52.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Tuesday: JPM, LLY, USB, PANW
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Tuesday as core CPI inflation came in slightly cooler than expected. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

U.S. Bancorp (USB) fell 1.34% in premarket trading after announcing a definitive agreement to acquire financial services firm BTIG for up to $1 billion. The purchase price is targeted at $725 million, comprising $362.5 million in cash and 6,600,594 shares of common stock.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) gained 0.42% before the opening bell after issuing fresh guidance for 2026. The bank now expects net interest income, excluding markets, of approximately $95 billion compared with $92.6 billion reported for 2025. Overall, the company anticipates NII of roughly $103 billion, market dependent, topping the Visible Alpha consensus of $100 billion.

Palo Alto Networks (PANW) slipped 0.28% in premarket trade following news that the Federal Trade Commission issued an early termination notice on its pending acquisition of Chronosphere. The Nikesh Arora-led cybersecurity firm announced its $3.35 billion deal to acquire Chronosphere in November 2025.

Eli Lilly (LLY) rose 0.46% in premarket trading after Chief Financial Officer Lucas Montarce said the pharma giant remains on track for a potential FDA approval of its obesity pill, orforglipron, in Q2 2026. In November, the FDA issued a National Priority Voucher for the company’s GLP-1 receptor agonist, designed as a small molecule.

data.title: Vision Marine posted Q1 revenue of $15.7M
data.description:
• Vision Marine Technologies (VMAR) for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, total revenues were $15.7 million, compared to $0.1 million in the prior-year period, driven primarily by the scale of the company's expanded U.S. retail platform following the inclusion of NVG's dealership operations.
  • Gross profit for the quarter totalled $4.2 million or 27%, reflecting the operating leverage of the expanded retail network.
  • The company generated $1.9 million of cash provided by operating activities, marking its first positive operating cash flow quarter following a period in which operations had historically consumed cash. 
  • As at November 30, 2025, Vision Marine reported cash of $2.3 million and a working capital surplus of about $4.7 million. These figures do not include the company's $9.6 million of gross proceeds of equity financing completed on December 19, 2025.
  • The company reported a net loss before taxes of $4.3 million for the quarter, compared to $1.1 million in the prior-year period. 
  • For the three-month period ended November 30, 2025, the company recorded an EBITDA loss of $2.3 million compared to $2 million in the prior-year period.
  • VMAR -2.428% premarket to $0.1447.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SEALSQ announces preliminary 2025 revenue and 2026 outlook
data.description:
• SEALSQ (LAES) said on Tuesday it posted preliminary unaudited revenue of $18 million for 2025, up 66% year on year, supported by renewed demand for legacy products and the consolidation of revenue from IC’ALPS following its August acquisition.

  • The semiconductor company said it recorded $8 million of revenue in the fourth quarter and ended the year with more than $425 million in cash, providing liquidity to support expansion across its quantum-security ecosystem.

  • SEALSQ reiterated its forecast for 2026 revenue growth of between 50% and 100% year on year, citing a full year of IC’ALPS revenue, new post-quantum chip launches and initial revenue from the Quantix Edge project.
  • Concensus for 2025 revenue is $17.95M.
  • The company said its business pipeline exceeds $200 million in potential opportunities from 2026 to 2028.
  • LAES +0.68% premarket to $4.38.
  • Source: Press Release
